metadata.vintage: Curated from each housing authority's published fee schedule + program guidelines. Cash-in-lieu rates + mitigation requirements change annually — verify on each authority's website before underwriting.
notes: Mountain resort markets concentrate around 60-150% AMI service-economy jobs
Mitigation programs trade development entitlements / fees for affordable units
Cash-in-lieu rates are typically the highest in the nation in CO resort markets
Deed restrictions enforce occupancy + maximum sale/lease pricing
Buy-down programs purchase open-market units to deed-restrict for workforce

authorities.name: Aspen-Pitkin County Housing Authority (APCHA)
authorities.headline: Most-active workforce-housing program in CO: ~3,000+ units, $700/sf cash-in-lieu, mandatory mitigation on commercial + residential

key_programs.name: Commercial Mitigation
key_programs.summary: Commercial development must mitigate housing demand it creates. Calculated as ~65% of new employees generated × per-employee floor area required. Met via build-on-site, buy-existing-and-deed-restrict, or cash-in-lieu.
key_programs.magnitude: Cash-in-lieu rates: ~$300-$700/sf of mitigated floor area depending on category 1-4 unit type. Among highest in US.

key_programs.name: Residential Affordable-Housing Requirement (RAH)
key_programs.summary: Free-market residential development requires affordable component (typically 70% of new floor area in housing-mitigation credits) OR equivalent cash payment.
key_programs.magnitude: Cash-in-lieu varies; large estates can owe $500K-$2M in RAH.

key_programs.name: APCHA Ownership Program
key_programs.summary: Categories 1-7 priced to income-qualified buyers (Cat 1 ~30% AMI through Cat 7 ~RO+200% AMI). Resale price-capped at indexed appreciation.
key_programs.magnitude: Cat 4 1BR (~120% AMI) prices around $350-$450K (vs $1.5M+ free-market comp).

key_programs.name: APCHA Rental Program
key_programs.summary: Income-qualified rental units across the Roaring Fork Valley. Long waitlist; tenants must work locally ≥1,500 hrs/yr.
key_programs.magnitude: Burlingame, Lumberyard, Centennial, dozens more — ~1,500+ rental units in portfolio.

developer_actions: Pitkin County developments require APCHA mitigation calculation early in entitlement
Master-lease agreements with APCHA can convert market-rate units to workforce inventory
Land donations to APCHA in lieu of mitigation are common on larger projects (recent: Lumberyard 277 units)

authorities.name: Town of Vail — InDEED Program + Vail Local Housing Authority
authorities.headline: Buy-down deed-restriction program: Town pays up to $200K per unit to deed-restrict existing market-rate condos as 'Vail InDEED' workforce housing

key_programs.name: Vail InDEED (existing-unit purchase)
key_programs.summary: Town pays property owner cash in exchange for recording a permanent deed restriction (occupancy + appreciation cap). Owner keeps full title.
key_programs.magnitude: Town pays ~10-25% of market value per unit. Original price + indexed appreciation governs future sales.

key_programs.name: Vail Commercial Linkage
key_programs.summary: Commercial development mitigates new employee housing demand via build/buy/fee.

key_programs.name: Timber Ridge Village (Town-owned rental)
key_programs.summary: Town's flagship rental portfolio — Vail Resorts master-leases for seasonal workforce. ~200 units; recently expanded.
key_programs.magnitude: Master-lease to Vail Resorts; income limits + work-locally requirements.

key_programs.name: Chamonix Vail (ownership)
key_programs.summary: Town-developed for-sale workforce condos. ~32 units, ~$400-$650K depending on bedroom count (vs $1.5M+ free-market West Vail).

developer_actions: Developers can sell existing market-rate inventory into InDEED for immediate cash + ongoing tax benefit
New commercial entitlement requires housing mitigation calculation
Town frequently joint-ventures with developers on land contributions

authorities.name: Eagle County / Valley Home Store + Eagle Valley Workforce Housing
authorities.service_area: Eagle County (Vail / Edwards / Eagle / Gypsum / Avon / Beaver Creek)
authorities.headline: County-level program; Valley Home Store operates ~1,200 deed-restricted ownership + rental units across the I-70 corridor

key_programs.name: Eagle County Deed Restriction Program
key_programs.summary: Similar to Vail InDEED but for unincorporated Eagle County + smaller towns. County buys deed restrictions on market units.
key_programs.magnitude: Per-unit cash payment varies; tied to county Affordable Housing Fund.

key_programs.name: Smart Land Use Initiative
key_programs.summary: Streamlined entitlement + density bonus for projects committing affordability covenants.

developer_actions: Edwards + Eagle + Gypsum entitlements increasingly require affordability commitments
Valley Home Store administers waitlists + qualification for resort workforce

authorities.name: Summit Combined Housing Authority (SCHA)
authorities.service_area: Summit County + all incorporated towns (Breckenridge, Frisco, Silverthorne, Dillon, Blue River, Montezuma)
authorities.headline: Multi-jurisdictional housing authority combining county + 4 towns + Vail Resorts; ~2,300+ deed-restricted units

key_programs.name: Mortgage Assistance Program
key_programs.summary: 5-Year + 20-Year Recapture options for workforce homebuyers. Covers up to $25K-$50K depending on income/employer tier.

key_programs.name: Long-Term Rental Program (LTR)
key_programs.summary: Property owners can voluntarily deed-restrict short-term-rental-eligible units into long-term rental in exchange for cash incentives. Counters STR conversion of workforce inventory.

key_programs.name: Wellington (Breckenridge)
key_programs.summary: Town-of-Breckenridge-built workforce ownership; recent phase ~150 units.

key_programs.name: Smith Ranch (Silverthorne)
key_programs.summary: Town-of-Silverthorne workforce community; ~140 units mixed rental + ownership.

developer_actions: Summit linkage requirements apply to commercial + larger residential entitlements
Town-of-Breckenridge has the most active land donation history in the county

authorities.name: Town of Telluride + San Miguel Regional Housing Authority
authorities.service_area: Telluride + Mountain Village + unincorporated San Miguel County
authorities.headline: Per-square-foot mitigation is among the highest in CO. Mandatory inclusionary on commercial + residential.

key_programs.name: Telluride Affordable Housing Mitigation
key_programs.summary: Commercial + residential development must mitigate new housing demand. Mitigation generated per sf of new development.

key_programs.name: Virginia Place + Pine Street Place (rental)
key_programs.summary: Town-developed workforce rental. Resort employee priority.

developer_actions: Mitigation calculations done by town housing director — engage in pre-application phase
Mountain Village has its own additional mitigation requirements

authorities.name: Estes Park Housing Authority
authorities.service_area: Town of Estes Park + unincorporated Larimer County around RMNP
authorities.headline: Resort-gateway town with year-round + seasonal workforce demand from Rocky Mountain National Park + YMCA of the Rockies

key_programs.name: Talons Pointe + Vista Ridge (rental)
key_programs.summary: Town-developed workforce rentals serving park + tourism workforce. Long waitlists.

key_programs.name: Estes Park linkage + inclusionary requirements
key_programs.summary: Selective inclusionary requirements on commercial + larger residential developments.

developer_actions: Engagement with Estes Park Housing Authority during entitlement is key
Town has worked with developers on land contributions for resort workforce projects

authorities.name: Yampa Valley Housing Authority (Routt County)
authorities.headline: Active local housing authority; Steamboat has rapidly-growing inventory through 2026

key_programs.name: Brown Ranch (planning)
key_programs.summary: 534-acre property acquired by YVHA in 2023; planned for ~2,400 workforce units over 20 years. Voter-approved annexation in progress.

key_programs.name: Steamboat Commercial Linkage
key_programs.summary: Commercial development mitigation requirements.

developer_actions: Brown Ranch is the largest workforce-housing pipeline in the state; partnerships welcomed
Routt County partners on land-contribution deals
